question.text: An insider submits a Trading Plan on January 1. The closing price of the share on December 31 (previous day) was Rs. 100. What is the maximum upper price limit the insider can set for a buy trade under the plan?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per Regulation 5(2)(iii)(iv)(a), for a buy trade, the upper price limit shall be up to twenty per cent higher than the closing price. 20% of Rs. 100 = Rs. 20, so upper limit = Rs. 120. **The Trap:** 10% or 30% may be confused with circuit limits or other thresholds. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If the market price exceeds the upper limit set by the insider, the trade simply will not be executed.
